title: Introduction to Copilot Spaces
objectives: Understand Copilot Spaces and when to use them
Create and manage Spaces with proper governance

sections.title: What is a Copilot Space?
sections.body:
A GitHub Copilot Space is a dedicated Copilot chat grounded in a curated set of context you choose. A Space acts like a focused LLM, and you can feed it GitHub files, issues, pull requests, and free-text instructions to provide context for your specific topic.

**When to Use Spaces** — Use Spaces when you want consistent, reproducible answers on a tightly scoped topic like a service, runbook, or dataset. Spaces trade breadth for depth: narrowing context to what matters most produces more predictable, grounded responses.

sections.title: Setting Context and Creating Spaces
sections.body:
**Setting Context for Spaces**
- Attaching files (uploads)
- Adding free-text instructions
- Linking GitHub issues and pull requests
- Specifying repository references

**Creating Your First Space**
1. Define the purpose and narrow boundaries
2. Choose high-signal context sources only
3. Add clear free-text instructions
4. Set ownership and maintenance responsibility
5. Share with appropriate team members
6. Document how to use the Space effectively

sections.title: Prompting in Spaces for Reliable Outputs
sections.body:
Effective prompting in Spaces follows patterns:
1. Confirm intent explicitly
2. Add concrete constraints (formats, ranges, file paths)
3. Reference attached sources by name
4. Request executable outputs with source references

This ensures grounded, verifiable, source-traceable responses.

sections.title: Governance, Security, and Scale
sections.body:
**Security & Access** — Spaces mirror GitHub permissions. No new access granted. Only surfaces what viewers can already see.

**Scale Management** — Oversized Spaces with low-signal context degrade precision. Split into single-purpose Spaces when scope becomes too broad.

**Branch-Specific Content** — Use narrow file references and add examples, or attach text files with exact content for historical snapshots.

sections.title: Exercise: Spaces for School Operations Knowledge
sections.body:
### Real-World Use Case
Business Context:
School procedures are spread across many files (attendance policy, grading rules, parent communication guide). New developers struggle to find consistent answers quickly.

Developer Task:
Create a Copilot Space with school operation documents and use it to produce grounded implementation guidance for APIs.

Example Input:
- Attached files: attendance-policy.md, grading-rules.md, parent-notification.md
- Prompt: "Using attached rules, propose validation for attendance update API and required notifications."

Expected Output:
- Answer cites attached sources.
- Clear validation steps and notification rules.
- Escalation guidance for exceptional cases.

Why This Matters:
Centralized knowledge helps teams build features faster and with consistent logic.

GitHub Copilot Challenge:
Ask Copilot in the Space to generate a checklist endpoint and force source citations; refine scope if answers become vague.

exercise.title: Build a school-operations Copilot Space
steps: Create a Space for school operations API guidance.
Define response format and citation rules.
Test prompts for attendance and grading scenarios.
Remove low-signal files if answers are generic.
troubleshooting: If responses are vague, narrow the Space topic.
If citations are missing, require section references.
If answers conflict, remove outdated docs.

quiz.question: When is a Copilot Space the better choice than general repo-wide chat?
options: When you need broad discovery across many repositories
When you want consistent, reproducible answers on a tightly scoped topic
When you want Copilot to automatically discover any relevant content
When you have no specific task in mind
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?

quiz.question: Which statement about Space ownership is true?
options: Spaces must be organization-owned; personal Spaces aren't supported
The description changes Copilot's answers in the Space
You can choose personal or organization ownership, and descriptions are for human readers
Ownership can't be changed and descriptions affect answer quality
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?

quiz.question: Which action does NOT add usable context in a Space?
options: Attaching files or folders from a repository
Pasting URLs of GitHub issues and pull requests
Uploading a local text document or spreadsheet
@-mentioning a Copilot extension to run in Space chat
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?

quiz.question: How do Spaces handle security and access?
options: Spaces grant temporary read access to all linked repositories
Spaces mirror GitHub permissions and surface only what viewers can see
Spaces create copies of private content accessible to anyone with the link
Spaces require repo admins to approve each viewer manually
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?

quiz.question: For branch-specific guidance in a Space, what should you do?
options: Change the repository's default branch
Rely on general chat to retrieve older versions
Narrow references to relevant files or attach text files with exact content
Paste sensitive historical content into free-text notes
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?

quiz.question: Which prompting pattern best supports verifiable outputs?
options: Ask for summaries without constraints to keep it creative
Confirm intent, add concrete constraints, request executable outputs with references
Use many broad instructions to widen context
Avoid referencing attached sources
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?

quiz.question: You notice size warnings and vague answers. What's the best next step?
options: Add more examples to increase context
Reduce sources or split into smaller focused Spaces
Start @-mentioning people to pull in expertise
Turn off repository linking
quiz.discussionPrompt: How would you apply this concept to your own projects?
